title: A source state, a governing arrow, and a destination state — the complete atomic unit of Precept's fire operation. The smaller source block is where the entity came from; the bright green arrow in the gap is the governing `->` operator; the larger destination block is where the entity arrives. In Precept, this triple is the only valid way to change state: no implicit transitions, no shortcuts, only declared paths.
conceptualBridge: The state-arrow-state triple means Precept's transition is a governed permission because the arrow in the gap is literally the `->` declaration in the definition — without it, no path connects the two states.
decision: Kept: 4.13 avg — the state-arrow-state triple successfully addresses the 53-two-states ambiguity; the arrow in the gap makes the governing permission explicit. Worth refining the proportions or simplifying further to boost the silhouette score.
